titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Dan Beach Bradley and Cornelius Beach Bradley Papers
Bradley (Dan Beach and Cornelius Beach) Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Nancy L. Fappiano

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 27, 2018 at 08:06
finding aid revisiion description not supplied

did.unittitle: Dan Beach and Cornelius Beach Bradley papers
did.abstract: Professional papers of Dan Beach Bradley and his son, Cornelius Beach Bradley, both missionaries to Thailand (then Siam) in the nineteenth century. The papers of Dan Beach Bradley consist of four manuscript volumes of a Siamo-English dictionary, articles and notes on the history and culture of Siam, including a list (copy) by the king of Siam of his 61 children in 1852, as well as copies of essays on the Siamese language in both English and Siamese by the king. A number of unidentified papers in Siamese, collected by Dan B. Bradley, are also included. His personal papers which contain 28 volumes of his journals (ca. 1835-1873), are in the library of Oberlin College. The journals have been filmed. Several maps of Thailand and China (1867-1870) have been sent to the Map Department of the Yale University Library. The Cornelius Beach Bradley papers consist chiefly of manuscripts on the Siamese language and rubbings of inscriptions on stone tablets accompanied by correspondence and notes on their translation. Other reprints relate to the origin of Siamese alphabetic writing and an analysis of the language. There is a small amount of correspondence for both Dan Beach and Cornelius Beach Bradley and one letter from Dan Beach Bradley (1767-1838) to his sister, Mehetabel, dated August 23, 1801.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Mrs. Harold C. Bradley, 1959.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Dan Beach and Cornelius Beach Bradley Papers (MS 97). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Dan Beach Bradley (1804-1873): missionary to Thailand; compiler of a Siamese-English dictionary.Cornelius Beach Bradley (1843-1936): missionary to Thailand. 1871-1874; professor at University of California at Berkeley, 1882-1911.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professional papers of Dan Beach Bradley and his son, Cornelius Beach Bradley, both missionaries to Thailand (then Siam) in the nineteenth century. The papers of Dan Beach Bradley consist of four manuscript volumes of a Siamo-English dictionary, articles and notes on the history and culture of Siam, including a list (copy) by the king of Siam of his 61 children in 1852, as well as copies of essays on the Siamese language in both English and Siamese by the king. A number of unidentified papers in Siamese, collected by Dan B. Bradley, are also included. His personal papers which contain 28 volumes of his journals (ca. 1835-1873), are in the library of Oberlin College. The journals have been filmed. Several maps of Thailand and China (1867-1870) have been sent to the Map Department of the Yale University Library. The Cornelius Beach Bradley papers consist chiefly of manuscripts on the Siamese language and rubbings of inscriptions on stone tablets accompanied by correspondence and notes on their translation. Other reprints relate to the origin of Siamese alphabetic writing and an analysis of the language. There is a small amount of correspondence for both Dan Beach and Cornelius Beach Bradley and one letter from Dan Beach Bradley (1767-1838) to his sister, Mehetabel, dated August 23, 1801.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The entire collection is available on microfilm. Patrons must use FILM HM 274 instead of the originals.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on use. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Inventory
ALS to Mehetabel (Bradley Bellamy) from her brother Dan Bradley (1767-1838)
Correspondence of Dan Beach Bradley (1804-1873)
Correspondence of Cornelius Beach Bradley
Receipts of Dr. Dan Beach Bradley (1804-1873)
Siamo-English dictionary. Vol. I
Siamo-English dictionary. Vol. II
Siamo-English dictionary. Vol. III
Siamo-English dictionary. Vol. IV
Psalms in Siamese
Two manuscripts [by Cornelius Beach Bradley?] concerning various aspects of the Siamese language
"The Siamese Vowels and Diphthongs," by Cornelius Beach Bradley
"Scheme for the Sequence of Words in a Siamese Vocabulary," by Cornelius Beach Bradley
"Indication of a Consonant-Shift in Siamese Since the Introduction of Alphabetical Writing" [reprint by Cornelius Beach Bradley]
"The Proximate Source of the Siamese Alphabet" [reprint by Cornelius Beach Bradley]
"The Siamese Vocabulary: Its Formal and Conceptual Features," by Cornelius Beach Bradley
"The Tone Accents of Two Chinese Dialects," by [Cornelius Beach Bradley]
"The Phyllotaxy of Phoenix Canariensis," by Cornelius Beach Bradley
"Taxonomy, or the Geometry of Leaf-Arrangement," by Cornelius Beach Bradley
Correspondence, photographs, and tracings of the inscriptions from Chayā
Reprints, transcripts, and transliteration by Cornelius Beach Bradley of the Sukhōthai inscription of Rām Khamhêng
"The History of the Sukhōthai Letters," by Cornelius Beach Bradley
"Graphic Analysis of the Tone-accents of the Siamese language," by Cornelius Beach Bradley
Miscellaneous research and data notes by Cornelius Beach Bradley
"A Book Publishing & Illustrating the evils of Opium...," by Dan Beach Bradley
"New Laws Concerning Business Transacted on Water and Land in Siam," by [Dan Beach Bradley]
Notes by [Dan Beach Bradley]: "Notes on Siamese Manners and Customs," "The Kingdom of Siam," and a "Synopsis of Siamese History"
Notes of Dan Beach Bradley pertaining to the Kingdom and King of Siam
Miscellaneous notes of Dan Beach Bradley pertaining to the Siamese language and Siamese books in the mission library
Unidentified notes and documents in Siamese either written or collected by Cornelius Beach and Dan Beach Bradley
Recipe for making wood oil and varnish and a recipe for a pain killer [written by Dan Beach Bradley?]
Issue of the Bangkok Times
Issue of the Bangkok Recorder
Photographs
Note cards of Siamese words
Tables for article on a Russian method of multiplication
From the papers of Cornelius Beach Bradley
Inscriptions from Wat Prasat and Wat Chieng Man, including photographs, transcriptions, transliterations and translations
Transliteration in modern Siamese letters of the four Sukhōthai inscriptions, following the original of Ram Khamkang, including photographs of the stone tablets
Studies of nothern [sic] writings, including rubbings, transcriptions, correspondence and photograph of a tablet
Pencil sketch
Tones of Cantonese [diagram]; Seven Test-Letters [chart]; correspondence; rubbings; unidentified materials
